response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to have a critical approach towards the information you encounter. Here are some steps that can help: 
Firstly, be aware of the sources of the information you consume on social media. Is it from a reputable news source or a random user? Take the time to verify the credibility of the source before sharing or believing the content. 
Secondly, be cautious of sensationalized headlines and clickbait articles. Often, these types of posts are designed to attract attention rather than provide accurate information. Take the time to read beyond the headline and understand the context of the article before sharing it with others. 
Thirdly, be mindful of the language used in social media posts. Is it inflammatory or intended to provoke a reaction? Be critical of language that is designed to manipulate your emotions rather than provide factual information. 
Finally, use fact-checking websites and tools to verify the accuracy of information you encounter on social media. These tools can help you identify misinformation and make informed decisions about what you choose to share or believe. By following these steps, you can be better equipped to manage and deal with fake content on social media in a responsible and critical manner.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: This answer is based on the persona of an 80-year-old man from Philadelphia, retired, with a vocational diploma. He has a critical approach towards information and is mindful of the language used on social media. He uses fact-checking websites to verify the accuracy of information and takes the time to read beyond the headline before sharing content.
